falsification_conditions.condition: S&P 500 closes below 4,800 on any single day within 14 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: A break below the August 2024 lows would invalidate the 79% bottom pattern match, as that prior bottom held above 5,100; this would confirm continuation lower not a structural bottom

falsification_conditions.condition: VIX closes above 35 for 3 consecutive days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: Persistent extreme fear above the August 2024 spike peak (~28) would signal a new panic regime, not the contained capitulation typical of a bottom; the blowoff score of 39 would be confirmed as insufficient for stabilization

falsification_conditions.condition: 10Y Treasury yield drops below 3.50% while S&P 500 simultaneously trades below its 50-day moving average
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: This flight-to-safety divergence would indicate recession pricing has taken hold, invalidating any bottom call by showing growth expectations are collapsing rather than stabilizing

doctrine: If you can't list what would prove your call wrong, you don't have a real call.
